column3: Li, D., Li, W., Lu, B., Li, H., Ma, S., Krishnan, G., & Wang, J.
column4: Delving deep into engagement prediction of short videos.
column6: How to model engagement levels purely from video content. This helps video creators and recommendation system even if there are limited initial user interactions
column7:
- Mean Opinion Scores (MOS) 
- Normalized Average Watch Percentage (NAWP)
- Engagement Continuation Rate (ECR)
column10:
- The MOS from traditional video quality assessment datasets do not correlate with actual video engagement levels. High visual quality does not necessarily mean high popularity

- NAWP is better to make the engagement of the prediction better
- ECR also helps the likelihood of users continuing to watch the video
column11: The study finds that combining features from visual content, background music, and text helps enhances the accuracy of engagement prediction compared to just the visuals alone
column13: Dataset used: 90,000 real-world UGC short videos from Snapchat
column15: Li, D., Li, W., Lu, B., Li, H., Ma, S., Krishnan, G., & Wang, J. (2024, September). Delving deep into engagement prediction of short videos. In European Conference on Computer Vision (pp. 289-306). Cham: Springer Nature Switzerland.

column3: Kai Zhao, Kun Yuan, Ming Sun and Xing Wen Kuaishou Technology
column4: Zoom-VQA: Patches, Frames and Clips Integration for Video Quality Assessment
column6: The purpose of the study is to simulate human perception of video quality based on Patches, Frames and Clips and not just factoring single frames of the video.
column8: They have their own model: https://github.com/k-zha14/Zoom-VQA
column10: The study found that different levels of the video reveal specific quality issues: motion blur is best reflected at the clip level, semantic information at the frame level, and local artifacts/noise at the patch level.

column4: LMM-VQA: Advancing Video Quality Assessment with Large Multimodal Models

column10: The framework successfully generated both a numerical quality score and a descriptive quality level (good, fair, poor)
